org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.commandbase.subsystems.swerve.encoders

Class OctoQuadSwerveEncoder

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.commandbase.subsystems.swerve.encoders.OctoQuadSwerveEncoder
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    SwerveEncoder

    public class OctoQuadSwerveEncoder
extends java.lang.Object
implements SwerveEncoder
    OctoQuad-based swerve encoder implementation.

    This class wraps the OctoQuad Firmware v3 encoder processor for use with swerve modules. It provides high-precision digital encoder readings with CRC validation.

    Encoder Configuration:

    Assumes REV Through-Bore encoders with:

    • Steering: 8192 counts per revolution (absolute positioning)
    • Drive: Velocity in ticks/second

    Port Mapping:

    The OctoQuad has 8 encoder ports. Typical swerve configuration:

    • Ports 0-3: Steering encoders (one per module)
    • Ports 4-7: Drive wheel encoders (one per module)

    Performance:

    • Update Rate: Up to 1000Hz (1kHz)
    • Resolution: 8192 counts/rev (0.044 degrees per count)
    • Accuracy: High with CRC validation
    See Also:
    SwerveEncoder, OctoQuadFWv3

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      private double cachedVelocityTicksPerSec
      Cached velocity reading (ticks/second).
      private int countsPerRev
      Counts per revolution for the encoder.
      private boolean isSteeringEncoder
      Whether this encoder is a steering encoder (true) or drive encoder (false).
      private OctoQuadFWv3 octoQuad
      OctoQuad encoder processor instance.
      private double offsetRadians
      Encoder offset calibration value (radians).
      private int port
      OctoQuad port number for this encoder.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      OctoQuadSwerveEncoder(OctoQuadFWv3 octoQuad, int port)
      Creates an OctoQuad-based drive encoder.
      OctoQuadSwerveEncoder(OctoQuadFWv3 octoQuad, int port, double offsetRadians, int countsPerRev)
      Creates an OctoQuad-based steering encoder.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      double getCachedVelocity()
      Gets the cached velocity reading (ticks/second).
      java.lang.String getEncoderType()
      Gets the encoder type for telemetry/debugging.
      double getPosition()
      Reads the current position from the encoder.
      double getVelocity()
      Reads the current velocity from the encoder.
      void reset()
      Resets the encoder to zero.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Field Detail

      • octoQuad

        private final OctoQuadFWv3 octoQuad
        OctoQuad encoder processor instance.

      • port

        private final int port
        OctoQuad port number for this encoder.

      • offsetRadians

        private final double offsetRadians
        Encoder offset calibration value (radians). Only used for steering encoders to set the zero position.

      • countsPerRev

        private final int countsPerRev
        Counts per revolution for the encoder. Default: 8192 for REV Through-Bore encoder.

      • isSteeringEncoder

        private final boolean isSteeringEncoder
        Whether this encoder is a steering encoder (true) or drive encoder (false). Steering encoders return position in radians, drive encoders return velocity in ticks/sec.

      • cachedVelocityTicksPerSec

        private double cachedVelocityTicksPerSec
        Cached velocity reading (ticks/second). Updated each time getVelocity() is called.

    • Constructor Detail

      • OctoQuadSwerveEncoder

        public OctoQuadSwerveEncoder(OctoQuadFWv3 octoQuad,
                             int port,
                             double offsetRadians,
                             int countsPerRev)
        Creates an OctoQuad-based steering encoder.
        Parameters:
        octoQuad - OctoQuad Firmware v3 instance
        port - OctoQuad port number (0-3 for steering)
        offsetRadians - encoder offset for zero calibration (radians)
        countsPerRev - counts per revolution (8192 for REV Through-Bore)

      • OctoQuadSwerveEncoder

        public OctoQuadSwerveEncoder(OctoQuadFWv3 octoQuad,
                             int port)
        Creates an OctoQuad-based drive encoder.
        Parameters:
        octoQuad - OctoQuad Firmware v3 instance
        port - OctoQuad port number (4-7 for drive)

    • Method Detail

      • getPosition

        public double getPosition()
        Description copied from interface: SwerveEncoder
        Reads the current position from the encoder.

        For steering encoders, this returns the absolute angle in radians.

        For drive encoders, this returns position in ticks (if supported).

        Specified by:
        getPosition in interface SwerveEncoder
        Returns:
        position in appropriate units (radians for steering, ticks for drive)

      • getVelocity

        public double getVelocity()
        Description copied from interface: SwerveEncoder
        Reads the current velocity from the encoder.

        For drive encoders, this returns velocity in ticks per second.

        For steering encoders, this may return 0 if velocity is not available.

        Specified by:
        getVelocity in interface SwerveEncoder
        Returns:
        velocity in appropriate units (typically ticks/second)

      • reset

        public void reset()
        Description copied from interface: SwerveEncoder
        Resets the encoder to zero.

        Not all encoders support this (absolute encoders cannot be reset).

        Specified by:
        reset in interface SwerveEncoder

      • getEncoderType

        public java.lang.String getEncoderType()
        Description copied from interface: SwerveEncoder
        Gets the encoder type for telemetry/debugging.
        Specified by:
        getEncoderType in interface SwerveEncoder
        Returns:
        encoder type name (e.g., "OctoQuad", "Analog")

      • getCachedVelocity

        public double getCachedVelocity()
        Gets the cached velocity reading (ticks/second).

        Useful for telemetry to avoid redundant I2C reads.

        Returns:
        cached velocity in ticks/second
